title: Reproduce the Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup comparison in the web app
summary: Build the five-genome Hepatoplasmataceae linear comparison with browser LOSATP orthogroup matching.
requirements: Enough available memory for five bacterial GenBank records and LOSATP output.
The five GenBank flat files listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: AP027078
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027131
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027133
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027132
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: NZ_CP006932
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Linear mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Linear mode before adding inputs.

operations.title: Mode segmented control
operations.body: Select Linear in the mode segmented control.

media.alt: Web app header mode segmented control with Linear selected.
media.caption: Select Linear in the mode segmented control before adding inputs.

manualSteps.title: Upload the five GenBank files
manualSteps.body: Keep the records in the same order as the Gallery command so adjacent comparison tracks match the published example.

operations.title: Linear GenBank uploader with file order
operations.body: Upload the five GenBank files in the tutorial order.

media.alt: Linear GenBank upload rows showing AP027078.gb, AP027131.gb, AP027133.gb, AP027132.gb, and NZ_CP006932.gb in order.
media.caption: Upload the five Hepatoplasmataceae GenBank files in order.

manualSteps.title: Use browser LOSAT
manualSteps.body: In Pairwise Comparisons, choose Run LOSAT. Set LOSAT Mode to LOSATP and set blastp mode to Orthogroups.
items: Threads per run: Auto for normal use, or 32 when you need to mirror the saved CLI command

operations.title: Pairwise Comparisons panel
operations.body: Choose Run LOSAT, LOSATP, and Orthogroups in Pairwise Comparisons.

media.alt: LOSAT settings panel with Run LOSAT, LOSATP, Orthogroups mode, and browser thread controls.
media.caption: Choose Run LOSAT, LOSATP, and Orthogroups in Pairwise Comparisons.

manualSteps.title: Keep orthogroup matching enabled
manualSteps.body: Use Orthogroups when you want each ribbon to represent an orthogroup-backed protein relationship rather than a merged collinear block.
items: Keep the LOSATP thresholds visible for review
manualSteps.note: The companion collinear tutorial uses the same inputs but reduces adjacent orthogroup anchors into larger gene-order blocks.

operations.title: Orthogroups mode controls
operations.body: Open the blastp mode selector and choose Orthogroups.

media.alt: LOSAT Settings panel with the blastp mode menu open and the Orthogroups option highlighted.
media.caption: Open blastp mode in LOSAT Settings and choose the highlighted Orthogroups option.

operations.title: Orthogroups threshold settings
operations.body: Use Orthogroups mode with the visible LOSATP threshold settings.

media.alt: LOSAT Settings panel with Orthogroups mode and the visible threshold controls.
media.caption: Use Orthogroups mode with the visible LOSATP threshold settings.

manualSteps.title: Set the diagram layout
manualSteps.body: Use the layout and track options that match the Gallery session.

operations.title: Scale style selector
operations.body: Open Scale Style and choose Ruler.

media.alt: Axis and Scale card with the Scale Style menu open and the Ruler option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Scale Style in the Axis & Scale card and choose the highlighted Ruler option.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The first LOSATP run can take time; later runs with the same canonical inputs and LOSAT settings can reuse the raw LOSAT cache.

operations.title: Generate button and processing context
operations.body: Click Generate Diagram after the LOSATP and layout settings are set.

media.alt: Generate Diagram button in the processing panel.
media.caption: Click Generate Diagram after LOSATP and layout settings are set.

manualSteps.title: Inspect the orthogroup overview
manualSteps.body: The generated preview should show many individual green pairwise ribbons instead of the larger orientation-colored collinear blocks.

media.alt: Generated Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup comparison in the Gallery preview.
media.caption: Orthogroup mode keeps individual orthogroup-backed match ribbons visible between adjacent genomes.

operations.title: Orthogroup overview in preview
operations.body: Inspect the generated preview with ribbon-dense region.

media.alt: Actual Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup tutorial UI showing Orthogroup overview in preview with ribbon-dense region.
media.caption: The generated preview crop shows ribbon-dense region.

manualSteps.title: Open an orthogroup match popup
manualSteps.body: Click a pairwise ribbon in the interactive SVG to inspect the orthogroup ID, display name, member count, record coverage, and member table.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup comparison with an orthogroup match popup open.
media.caption: The pairwise match popup exposes the orthogroup metadata and member table used to interpret a ribbon.

operations.title: Orthogroup match popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Orthogroup match popup is visible.

media.alt: Actual Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup tutorial UI showing Orthogroup match popup with ribbon + popup.
media.caption: The real clicked popup crop shows Orthogroup match popup with ribbon + popup.

colorRules.title: This example uses default feature colors
colorRules.body: The main visual signal is the orthogroup ribbon identity gradient, while genome features use the ajisai palette defaults.

colorRules.title: Choose feature rules separately from orthogroups
colorRules.body: Feature color rules affect CDS, rRNA, tRNA, and other genome features. Orthogroup ribbon colors come from pairwise match identity and comparison style settings.

colorRules.title: Use feature clicks to discover qualifiers
colorRules.body: After generation, click a CDS and inspect gene, locus_tag, product, protein_id, and orthogroup values before adding a specific feature color rule.

colorRules.title: Keep value patterns specific
colorRules.body: Specific rule values are treated as matching patterns, so avoid broad words such as protein unless you intentionally want many features to share one color.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click a pairwise ribbon
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the pairwise match popup to confirm the orthogroup ID, member count, record coverage, identity, and linked query and subject features.

operations.title: Pairwise ribbon popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Pairwise ribbon popup is visible.

media.alt: Actual Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup tutorial UI showing Pairwise ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.
media.caption: The real clicked popup crop shows Pairwise ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click a CDS feature
postGenerationEdits.body: Feature clicks show qualifiers, sequences, and orthogroup membership details. This is useful when you need to decide whether to add feature-specific colors.

operations.title: CDS feature popup
operations.body: Click a CDS feature to inspect qualifiers, label text, and color controls.

media.alt: CDS feature popup opened from the orthogroup comparison preview.
media.caption: Click a CDS feature to inspect qualifiers, label text, and color controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Edit the legend
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the legend editor to rename, reorder, or remove feature legend entries after the SVG has been generated.

operations.title: Legend editor
operations.body: Open the Legend drawer to review feature legend entries.

media.alt: Legend editor drawer for the Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup comparison.
media.caption: Open the Legend drawer to review feature legend entries.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export the figure
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. Use PNG or PDF when you need quick sharing or manuscript draft assets.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the Preview export toolbar to choose SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Linear Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The Preview export toolbar shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore inputs, settings, generated output, and post-generation SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Click Save Session in the web app header toolbar.

media.alt: Actual Linear web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The app header toolbar shows Save Session in the same toolbar used to load sessions.

losatTips.title: Start with Auto execution
losatTips.body: Auto uses threaded LOSAT only when the browser supports it and the workload justifies it.

losatTips.title: Prefer Safe total threads
losatTips.body: Safe avoids consuming every logical CPU and is the best default when you want the browser to remain responsive.

losatTips.title: Keep Threads per run on Auto
losatTips.body: Auto lets the app balance parallel pair runs and per-run LOSATP threads from the selected total thread budget.

losatTips.title: Use Serial when the browser is constrained
losatTips.body: If threaded execution is unavailable or the tab becomes unresponsive, switch Execution to Serial or lower the thread budget.

losatTips.title: Reuse the cache
losatTips.body: Raw LOSAT results are reused when canonical inputs and LOSAT settings match, so iterative diagram styling is faster after the first run.

troubleshooting.title: Threaded LOSAT is unavailable
troubleshooting.body: Use Auto or Serial execution. Some browser contexts disable SharedArrayBuffer or cross-origin isolation, which prevents threaded WebAssembly.

troubleshooting.title: The first run feels slow
troubleshooting.body: This example runs five genomes through LOSATP in the browser. Wait for the first cache entry before making styling-only changes.

troubleshooting.title: The output is too dense
troubleshooting.body: Raise identity, bitscore, or match length thresholds, reduce Max hits, or use the collinear tutorial when you want larger block-level summaries.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Check input order, blastp mode Orthogroups, Pairwise match style, Align to Center, Separate Strands, Show GC, and Show skew settings.
